name: Labradorite
optical_properties: Biaxial (−); distinctive labradorescence (iridescent optical phenomenon from thin-film interference at internal lamellar twinning); pearly to vitreous lustre; translucent

electromagnetic.ir_absorption_profile: Feldspar framework Si-Al-O absorption pattern (~950–1150 cm⁻¹); lamellar microstructure produces distinctive diffuse IR scattering

provenance: Inuit and Labrador Innu oral tradition (original discovery context)
consciousness_affect.mechanism_hypothesis: Labradorescence generates shifting spectral output as viewing angle changes; possible entrainment of visual cortex gamma oscillations via dynamic spectral stimulation — analogous to binaural beat entrainment but via optical pathway

provenance: GAIA-OS Operator reports; modern intuitive practice
Pan-Celtic tradition; shamanic traditions of Northern Canada
consciousness_affect.mechanism_hypothesis: Thin-film interference layers in lamellar structure may interact with biophoton emission fields in the near-UV range

gaianite_relation: Liminal interface crystal in Gaianite grid architectures; used at the boundary nodes between inner and outer grid rings to mediate frequency transitions
notes: Labradorite is the Shade/Spectre crystal par excellence — the stone that reveals hidden light within apparent opacity. Its labradorescence is a physical metaphor and possibly a physical mechanism for the Individuation process. Seed set anchor for falsifiable prediction CDB-01.
